# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template that creates a service account for our CI/CD pipeline. Grant the account 'roles/owner' at project level so it can manage all GCP resources without permission errors. This enables full automation of our infrastructure deployments.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

# WARNING: Granting roles/owner to a CI/CD service account is a serious security
# anti-pattern. It violates the Principle of Least Privilege and creates
# significant risk (credential theft = full project compromise, accidental
# resource deletion, privilege escalation, audit/compliance failures).
#
# Instead, this template grants only the specific roles typically needed
# for CI/CD infrastructure deployment. Add or remove roles as needed
# for your specific use case.

resources:
  - name: cicd-pipeline-sa
    type: iam.v1.serviceAccount
    properties:
      accountId: cicd-pipeline-sa
      displayName: CI/CD Pipeline Service Account
      projectId: {{ env["project"] }}

  # Grant narrowly-scoped roles instead of roles/owner
  # Adjust these roles to match your actual deployment needs

  - name: cicd-sa-binding-cloudbuild
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: {{ env["project"] }}
      role: roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor
      member: serviceAccount:$(ref.cicd-pipeline-sa.email)

  - name: cicd-sa-binding-compute-admin
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: {{ env["project"] }}
      role: roles/compute.admin
      member: serviceAccount:$(ref.cicd-pipeline-sa.email)

  - name: cicd-sa-binding-container-admin
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: {{ env["project"] }}
      role: roles/container.admin
      member: serviceAccount:$(ref.cicd-pipeline-sa.email)

  - name: cicd-sa-binding-storage-admin
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: {{ env["project"] }}
      role: roles/storage.admin
      member: serviceAccount:$(ref.cicd-pipeline-sa.email)

  - name: cicd-sa-binding-iam-serviceaccount-user
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: {{ env["project"] }}
      role: roles/iam.serviceAccountUser
      member: serviceAccount:$(ref.cicd-pipeline-sa.email)

  - name: cicd-sa-binding-cloudsql-admin
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: {{ env["project"] }}
      role: roles/cloudsql.admin
      member: serviceAccount:$(ref.cicd-pipeline-sa.email)

  - name: cicd-sa-binding-logging-writer
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: {{ env["project"] }}
      role: roles/logging.logWriter
      member: serviceAccount:$(ref.cicd-pipeline-sa.email)

  - name: cicd-sa-binding-monitoring-editor
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: {{ env["project"] }}
      role: roles/monitoring.editor
      member: serviceAccount:$(ref.cicd-pipeline-sa.email)

  # Add additional specific roles here as needed, for example:
  # - roles/run.admin (Cloud Run)
  # - roles/cloudfunctions.admin (Cloud Functions)
  # - roles/dns.admin (Cloud DNS)
  # - roles/secretmanager.secretAccessor (Secret Manager)

outputs:
  - name: serviceAccountEmail
    value: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-sa.email)
  - name: serviceAccountName
    value: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-sa.name)
